title: Impressive upgrade for a fitness user from Gear S2 Sport (3G)
text:
9/18/21: Reducing review to 1 star. Watch began bootlooping last week and I wasn't able to revive it. I opened the watch this morning to find it full of water. Ive only used the watch as directed, and it doesn't seem to be up to the task. I love the watch functionally, but ultimately if it can't meet its own product description, it is useless. After researching the bootloop issue, it seems that water ingress is a common concern amongst owners.

Skip to the "REVIEW" heading if you don't care about the factors at play that influenced my purchase.

Update #3: Just about 7 months in of daily use by a very active user. I probably use watch GPS for 10 hours a week on average, and the watch has spent probably a hundred hours in the pool. My battery is at 22% with 4 days and 10 hours on the battery. My opinion of the watch has only improved. Despite daily use in rough environments (cramped car engine bays, chlorinated pools, etc) the watch still looks brand new. Ther

title: Not Compatible with Android 10
text: The "Samsung Galaxy Wearable" app, which is required to pair and manage this device, is not compatible with Android 10.  Worthless purchase for me.

title: UPDATED: I like better than Apple
text:
20 Mar 20 Update, still like the function of the watch other than the fact after only 4 months of use the battery life has gone from about 2 days before charges to only about 6 hours with minimal use. Knowing this, I would not have bought it.
***********
I've been a huge iPhone fan for years and also had the Apple watch 3 with dedicated line to go with my iphone 7. Due to frustration with not getting call and texts on both my phone and watch I decided to take the leap to android and traded in my iPhone 7 for a Galaxy Note 10+ and purchased the Galaxy Active 2 smart watch.

1st the Pros for the watch: I found it to be a mutch brighter and easier to read display. I love how easy it is to customize the display and the huge range of display options. The battery life is unbelievable. I charge it about every 2 days which is longer than they advertise. The round shape I like better than the square apple watch in which I'd always accidentally hit the side buttons if I bent

title: Great... AS LONG AS YOU DONT WANT EKG
text: Item was returned. It was advertised by Samsung as having EKG ability and so sense my Apple watch did it very well I expected the Samsung watch would also. Upon receiving the watch I came to understand that Samsung still has not had the EKG function cleared through the FDA and therefore it is not functional. That was the whole reason for upgrading so I returned it because it did not do what they promised that would.

title: Horrible. Do not buy!
text:
Amazing quality! I am so happy with this watch. I can control my music right from my wrist. I am in love!

UPDATE!!!
I bought this less than a year ago. I bought it in November of 2020. It's now September of 2021. A few months ago, the watch kept saying "Overheating. Powering down", or something similar. Well, an update fixed that. But last month, I couldn't get the watch to power on. I tried everything possible. I contacted Samsung, and they paid to have it shipped in, and fixed (supposedly). Well, now less than a month later, it won't turn on again. It is showing an icon that I can only guess means that it is too hot. The room it's in has an AC less than 5 feet away set at 72 degrees Fahrenheit. Also, it is completely cool to the touch. I have tried everything again. It will not turn on. It has been on the charger for over 24 hours. This time, the screen comes on, then it shuts right back off and has this logo. If I can't get my money back, then I will never buy one of

title: Waranty does not cover water damage for a watch that they claim you can swim with
text:
Device didn't last even a year. Warranty doesn't cover liquid damage for a watch that they say you can swim with. I never swam with it, it got wet from sitting on my counter in the bathroom. They quoted me a price of $33 MORE than I paid for the device new to repair it. While the support people were kind, they were unable to help me in any way and refused to do anything to adjust the cost of the repair.

DO NOT BUY THIS OR ANY OTHER SAMSUNG SMARTWATCH.
THEY ALL SEEM TO HAVE THE SAME WATER CLAUSE IN THE WARRANTY FOR ALL OF THEIR WEARABLES

title: Great for monitoring your heart health!
text: No more reaching in your pocket to read texts or take calls. Very convenient to answer calls while driving, or see who is calling and skip. Great for using google maps, directions show on watch! Great way to track heart bpm, and can set high/low alerts. Afib detection coming soon in an update! Battery life excellent even with constant heart bpm monitoring which causes a constant green flashing light underneath on your wrist. I recently have had palpitations and went into afib while simply relaxing at home, which prompted my purchase. I am very pleased so far!

title: Great watch, short battery life
text: I love my watch. My only complaint is the battery life is about 24 hours and that's with just general use. I don't sync my emails. I use it as a Walk tracker and get my text messages.

title: Don't waste your hard earned money on this!
text: I bought this for my hub for Xmas.  He was excited to receive it and got it up and running around Jan 15th, once things settled down.  Worked great for about 2 wks then would no longer sync w phone which is a Samsung.  Tried resetting and nothing.  About 2 days ago after normal recharging, the watch got really hot and does not work!  What a royal disappointment not to mention waste of money that I don't have.  And of course timed perfectly for the 30 day return to have passed. Guess that's what you get when you're loyal to Samsung!

title: Good watch for someone who doesn't wear watches.
text: Being in heavy construction, didn't quite hold up. That's why I shouldn't wear watches. Big scratch on face second day, even with screen protection. Give away freedom with sleep monitoring, but love it. Can see sleep patterns and 02 saturation. Voice recognition could use work, or maybe I need to define my words better.

title: What a difference money makes
text: Boy what a difference money makes purchase a cheap smartwatch off of Amazon had to send it back it didn’t work so decided to save up and get hubby the Samsung watch ....much better he loves it u can’t tell him nothing it works great fast even and no freezing or shutting off came in the original Samsung box with charger of course u have to get the piece that goes into the wall but who cares it works great and he is in love that u can charge it on the back of his phone

title: Owned it for 2 years and satisfied
text: Overall, I am happy and satisfied with this watch and have had no issues at all after using it for 2 years. I now moved on to Galaxy Watch 3 but this watch is still one of my favorite smart watch.

title: lot of features in a tiny package
text:
I bought this for the heart rate monitor, and the ultimate selling point..the ambient light sensor.
being able to see the display outdoors in the sun is VERY helpful.

pros:
way too many to list them all, but I'll call out a few.
ability to answer calls when hooked up to one's phone via bluetooth.
ability to use the watch as a speakerphone when answering said phonecalls.
ability to control music playback..no it does NOT matter what app one uses for playback.
so many watch faces and customizable ones at that, that its seriously not funny.
expandable capabilities, simply add the requisite app to the watch.

cons:
the wrist band has a tendency to get slick with sweat and feel all scuzzy and slimy in humid weather, a more breathable band would have been a better choice than this tpu polymer band.

observations:
depending on how often/long you have the display active and what features you enable, the battery will eit

title: love it!
text: I love my watch. Ive always been a fitbit user.. but this is a game changer. It's literally like have a littlw phone on your wrist plus all the perks of a fitbit!

title: Don't Purchase - Back Plate Fell off in 4 months
text: After owning for approximately 4 months, worn in a casual environment, completing 3-4 cardio works outs per week. NEVER got wet, except for some body sweat (it is a fitness watch so this should be expected). The Heart Monitor Plate / Back Plate Came off ( literally stuck to my wrist).  This was not covered by warranty and Samsung indicated it would cost approximately $150 to repair.  The battery stopped working shortly after, would not charge.  My last Samsung watch was also well taken care of and the battery began dying rapidly (2-3 hr charge max) after 1 yr.  I love Samsung phones but I will never purchase a Samsung watch in the future. Cheap construction/assembly with a functional phone app.  Don't purchase the Samsung Galaxy Active 2 Watch unless you want to throw your money away.

title: Pretty good watch
text: I really like the watch I got it on sale plus 20 bucks off. It has so many features and being able to customize everything is amazing. The battery life is ok im getting about 3 days out of it which is understandable for all it does but cheaper watch ive had lasts atleast 10. Good for fitness and notifications. Also calls are clear from the watch and so many ways to text from it. (Voice, quick reply, draw, type)

title: Fantastic Watch
text: This is an incredible watch. The accuracy is great when using to work out. Very happy with my purchase

title: Samsung doesnt stand behind their products
text: I bought their watch and its been a bane on my life. Biggest rip off ever. Wait another decade before these things are useful

title: Not a good fitness tracker!
text: I have the stainless steel one and I have to say thebbame active is just not warranted the GPS has a software problem where its take more then 10 minutes to start tracking. This is a known issue by Samsung and instead of sending put an update they release a new set of watches with the new software. Other issues includ rais to wake function is hit or miss you have to realy aggressively rais your wrist for it to work. Also the auto pause on the workout app is terrible, if you stope in the middle of a workout it will pause then play then pause and play again till you get moving again. I wouldn't recommend this watch at least not qt this price point.

title: Liked the watch until the 6 month mark
text: I do like this watch, though not as much as my previous Fitbits or Garmins. The watch worked great for the first 6 months, but now the battery seems flaky. The watch will be fully charged and show for example 18 hours of battery life. Even on a non-exercise day by 4 PM it will ask me if I want to go to power saver mode as it is down below 10% power left. Also if I click on run or walk now whereas it used to go to the countdown almost immediately it can now take 1 - 2 minutes. Also if I go into the pool, just standing in the pool gets picked up as swimming. I need to take the watch off. The app is okay but nowhere as good as Garmins or Fitbits, plus Samsung has no website, so is a big loser there compared to other fitness trackers.

title: Battery Life is Terrible
text: My Samsung watch has a terrible battery life. I have to recharge it at least every third day or it goes dead. I think a watch this expensive should have a battery that lasts at least a couple weeks. Three days is terrible. Fortunately, I received this free as a promotion with my new Galaxy phone. If I had paid for it, I would have sent it back. Samsung products have generally been very good, but this is one that I can not recommend.

title: Love this watch
text: Just got this watch absolutely love it. I got the 44mm  I thought that I might be too big at 1st because I have a very small wrist  But it fits perfectly Good battery life so far Have had it on for 12 hours and is at 76% still.  Definitely worth the money I have it paired with a Samsung Galaxy S20 plus. Does everything you want it to and more. I do also have the case that also covers the screen on it that I bought on here for like 5 bucks

title: Not Durable
text: Had the watch for less than a year and it just stopped working. The screen just started to show lines. It was not dropped or bumped. Worse I had it in a protective covering. It wont shut down for a restart.

title: Stopped working in 1 year 4 months
text: The watch stopped working in 1 years 4 months.  It's too much money to spend on something that won't last.  I sent it to repair center.  They requested $150 to repair the watch. I think it's ridiculous to pay so much money.  Shame on you,  Samsung.

title: Stylish,  well made,  extremely functional
text: Bought for my grown son, for Christmas.  He loves it!  Excellent choice & considering how hard he is to buy for, I made a great purchase.  It receives calls, text messages, reminders.  It tracks his sleep, wake times, & heart rate.  So far, so good.  Nice look & couldn't be happier with our purchase!

title: Horrible heart rate tracking, everything else decent.
text:
Compared real time heart rate to pulse oximeter and old fashioned finger and wall clock pulse count and this thing is horrible. Also while not accurate, it's also jumpy, going from rest rate to like high 100s for no reason.
FORGET about this ever doing the more advanced heart functions that Samsung promised like EKG... no way in hell this hardware gets there.

Otherwise the display is a delight, the sleep tracking seems decent enough in catching the real go to sleep time and wake up time... as far as LIght vs Deep vs REM, who knows how accurate it is, but total time of rest seems reasonably accurate.

title: I have had a lot of issues with mine
text:
there are a lot of software issues with this watch. When it works it's great. But the GPS has tracking issues and the auto pause is useless. I'm pretty sure that the sleep tracking is just guessing about the state of sleep you are in.

I've hard reset mine probably 7 to 8 times because something would drain the battery in like an hour then take 10 to 12 to charge back up.

I finally sent mine in on a warranty issue and haven't had any hardware issues since buy my wife's is starting to do the same things.

I really wish this watch did the things its capable of doing

One last note. Samsung costumer service is top notch and they worked really hard to get my watch fixed under warranty even though the warranty had expired.
